summary: Decent audience tape of a flat late‑80s set, highlighted by a lively Louie Louie jam but hampered by overall lackluster improvisation.
review: The recording is an audience source with clear sound and low crowd noise, making it easy to follow the performance. Musically the show feels average – Jerry was in good spirits and Brent showed more confidence on keys, yet many fans found the set thin, citing weak improvisation and a disappointing Stones > Not Fade Away ending. Louie Louie and the closing jam of Might As Well stand out as the few bright spots.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is generally viewed as flat to poor (average ~2.8/5) while recording quality is decent (~4/5). Weighted combination yields a moderate overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience tape with decent clarity and minimal crowd noise; no major drop‑outs or muffling reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set feels uneven – a few energetic moments (Louie Louie, ending jam) are offset by a general lack of improvisation and a weak closing sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Appears upbeat and healthy, contributing positively to the vibe.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent shows more confidence onstage, stepping out of his shell.
